---
title: "Data, Evidence, and Decision Making (with R)"
author: "Ayush Patel"
subtitle: "Session 1"
institute: "National Law School of India University, Bengaluru"
date: today
date-format: "DD MMM, YYYY"
embed-resources: true
bibliography: citations.bib
format: 
  revealjs:
    fig-height: 6
    fig-width: 14
    embed-resources: true
    margin-left: 50px
    margin-right: 50px
    slide-number: c/t
    width: 1400
    height: 850
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    footer: "Email: ayush.ap58@gmail.com"
---

# Why should I care about *math/numbers/data* ?

> Is this even useful?

## *Predict stuff* 


::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

[How much moeny will I make when I graduate?]{.fragment fragment-index='1'}
<br>  
[What will be the price of dollar against the rupee in the next fifteen days?]{.fragment fragment-index='2'}
<br>  
[Who will win the next general elections?]{.fragment fragment-index='3'}
<br>  
[Is this going to be a boring course?]{.fragment fragment-index='4'}

:::

::: {.column}

![[The Fortune Teller by Robert Loftin Newman](https://collectionapi.metmuseum.org/api/collection/v1/iiif/11654/36952/main-image)](https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/ad/original/ap21.159.1.jpg)

:::

:::

## *Understand stuff*

::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

[Why are some people more poor than other?]{.fragment fragment-index='1'}
<br>  
[Does the Indian judiciary have in-group bias? @aabbcdgns2025bias]{.fragment fragment-index='2'}
<br>  
[Is population growth slowing down?]{.fragment fragment-index='3'}
<br>  
[Can I make a confident estimate about all by looking at the few?]{.fragment fragment-index='4'}

:::

::: {.column}

![[Aristotle with a Bust of Homer by Rembrandt (Rembrandt van Rijn) ](https://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search/437394)](https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/ep/original/DP-30758-001.jpg)

:::

:::

# Essentially, we want to be right about stuff

> Lets do a small discussion. Consider the claim that is made in the following video.

## The claim

See [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-0X5qKkNNuw) from 1:10 to 2:01


> The Bihar government recruited X numbers of people for a certain position (police constable). The news anchor highlights that two coaching centers in Bihar claim that a total number A and B of their students respectively cleared the exam. She points out that A+B > X. Which implies there is something off about the claims of coaching centers.

## Assessing the Claim

<br>
[What is your initial intuition about the claim? Inclined to agree/diagree/unsure?]{.fragment fragment-index='1'}
<br>  
[Whatever way to lean, try to list out why it might be the correct choice]{.fragment fragment-index='2'}
<br>  
[Also list out why the other two choices are not possible?]{.fragment fragment-index='3'}
<br>  
[Do you *feel* the claim may or may not be true becasue a particular anchor or channel is presenting it?]{.fragment fragment-index='4'}

## Implicit Assumption

<br>
[Did you consider that it is possible for an individual to be enrolled in more than one coaching center? Especially if classes are online.]{.fragment fragment-index='1'}
<br>  
[Is passing the exam necessarily mean you will get an appointment letter?]{.fragment fragment-index='2'}
<br>  
[There even might be people that would have not taken up the job even after getting an appointment letter]{.fragment fragment-index='3'}

## Caring about math/numbers/data helps with

<br>
[Making sense of the world around us.]{.fragment fragment-index='1'}
<br>  
[Answering questions we care about]{.fragment fragment-index='2'}
<br>  
[Searching for patterns leads to more interesting questions. See Introduction in @spiegelhalter2019art]{.fragment fragment-index='3'}



# References
